description: drift-check Class-C skips non-scheduled crons/*.md by mirroring the runtime's schedulable-cron predicate (leading schedule: frontmatter, not enabled:false) before mtime-checking — killing the recurring crons/README.md 'inert' false positive.

userStories.title: Add a schedulable-cron predicate to drift-check Step C-2
userStories.description: As an operator running /drift-check, I want Class-C to evaluate only files the runtime actually loads and runs, so non-cron docs like crons/README.md are never flagged 'inert'.
acceptanceCriteria: In .claude/skills/drift-check/SKILL.md Step C-2, each crons/*.md file is qualified as a schedulable cron BEFORE the mtime comparison; non-qualifying files are skipped silently (no DRIFT-CHECK (C) line, not counted toward the inert aggregate).

Predicate qualifies a file IFF ALL hold: (1) first line is literally '---' after stripping a trailing \r (CRLF-safe); (2) a closing '---' delimiter exists on a later line; (3) within that leading frontmatter block a non-comment line matches '^[[:space:]]*schedule:' (anchored, excludes '# schedule:' and 'pre_schedule:'); (4) the block does not set enabled to false (tolerate false, "false", 'false').
The opening '---' MUST be the first line of the file so a '---' inside a fenced code block (the crons/README.md trap) cannot match.
Predicate is property-based, not name-based: no hard-coded cron count or name list in the skill logic.

Verified against the real tree: running the Step C-2 logic with RUNTIME_START set before the files' mtimes flags crons/cleanup-tasks.md, crons/eval-weekly.md, crons/heartbeat.md (and crons/autopilot.md while enabled:true) but NOT crons/README.md.
Snippet passes 'bash -n' syntax check and uses only awk/head/grep/stat (no new deps).
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update drift-check Output Contract wording to 'schedulable cron files'
userStories.description: As a reader of the skill, I want the documented Class-C output to describe what is actually evaluated, so the docs match the corrected behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The Output Contract section and any Step C-2/C-3 example output describe the inert-file set as 'schedulable cron files' (files passing the predicate), not bare crons/*.md, and make clear qualification is predicate-based, not name-based.

The aggregate 'DRIFT:' summary line 'cron-staleness drift (N inert file)' is described as counting only schedulable cron files, and any inline example shows a real cron such as crons/heartbeat.md, never crons/README.md.
No Output Contract text implies README.md or other non-scheduled files are evaluated or counted.
The '(C) Cron-staleness drift: OK' clean-token contract is unchanged.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add the drift-check-cron-staleness-glob eval probe
userStories.description: As the harness, I want a deterministic probe that extracts and runs the live Step C-2 logic against fixtures, so a future revert to the raw glob is genuinely caught.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/drift-check-cron-staleness-glob.sh exists, is executable, and begins with '#!/usr/bin/env bash', '# tier: A', '# source: issue #98', a '# desc:' line naming the extract-and-run strategy, and 'set -euo pipefail'; all diagnostics go to stderr and the final line is PASS:/REGRESSION:/SKIPPED:.

The probe EXTRACTS the Step C-2 bash block from the live .claude/skills/drift-check/SKILL.md (the fenced ```bash block following the '**Step C-2 — compare cron file mtimes:**' heading), echoes it to stderr, and exits 1 (REGRESSION) if extraction is empty or the block does not iterate crons/*.md.

The probe registers a trap-based temp-dir cleanup BEFORE creating fixtures, then builds three fixtures in a temp crons/ each with a fresh mtime: (a) README-style (H1 first line, no line-1 '---') -> must be EXCLUDED; (b) valid scheduled cron (leading '---' + schedule: + enabled:true + closing '---') -> must be INCLUDED; (c) disabled cron (schedule: present but enabled:false) -> must be EXCLUDED.

The probe runs the extracted predicate with RUNTIME_START set before the fixtures' mtimes and exits 0 (PASS) only when the inert set excludes (a) and (c) AND includes (b); exits 1 (REGRESSION) otherwise; exits 2 (SKIPPED) only if SKILL.md or stat is genuinely absent.
REGRESSION wiring demonstrated once during implementation: running the probe against a Step C-2 block reverted to the raw 'for f in crons/*.md' glob makes it exit 1; note this in progress.txt.
bash evals/probes/drift-check-cron-staleness-glob.sh exits 0 against the fixed skill; shellcheck -S warning is clean on the new probe.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
